name: Wisconsin Cassoulet
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces dried navy beans, picked clean
3 boned/skinned chicken breast halves, cut into 1 1/2-inch pcs
2 ounces slab bacon, cut into 1/4-inch dice
1 1/2 pounds pork tenderloin, cut into 1 1/2-inch pcs
14-oz can stewed red ripe tomatoes, undrained & chopped
1 1/2 tablespoons flat leaf parsley, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the beans overnight in water. Rinse several times in cold water and drain.
2. Place the soaked beans in a large heavy pot with fresh cold water to cover them.
3. Add celery tops, bay leaves, and parsley sprigs. Simmer over medium heat for 30 minutes.
4. In a large pot, brown the bacon over medium heat until the fat renders. Remove the bacon with a slotted spoon and set aside.
5. Add one tablespoon of olive oil to the pot. Brown the chicken and pork in small batches, then remove them to a bowl using a slotted spoon.
6. Add the remaining tablespoon of oil to the pot. Wilt the onion, celery, and carrots over medium-low heat for 8-10 minutes.
7. Return the meats and bacon to the pot with the beans (discard celery tops).
8. Add all remaining ingredients through cracked black pepper; simmer gently for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
9. Stir in the kielbasa and simmer for an additional 30 minutes, stirring once or twice.
10. Stir in the chopped parsley and season with salt. Serve immediately, garnished with the remaining parsley.
